extras.content: Similar advances with other crops have released people from hard and boring jobs for more creative work, whilst machines harvest crops more efficiently with less waste. 译文:对其他农作物所做的类似改进使得人们可以远离脏活累活，从而转向那些更富有创造性的工作，同时由机器收割庄稼可以提高效率、降低损耗。

senses.def_en: to give up (a job or position) in a formal or official way
senses.example: I resigned because of the low salary.
senses.example_zh: 因为工资太低，我辞职了。

senses.def_en: to make yourself accept sth. that is bad but cannot be changed (~to)
senses.example: resign oneself to one's fate
senses.example_zh: 听天由命

senses.example: They surrendered to the enemy.
senses.example_zh: 他们向敌人投降了。

senses.def_zh: (~to)屈服(于)，听任(感情、习惯等)摆布
senses.example: surrender to temptation
senses.example_zh: 屈服于诱惑

senses.example: No surrender to the fascists!
senses.example_zh: 决不向法西斯分子投降！

senses.def_en: to let sb. go free; to let a substance flow out
senses.example: release the hostages // Plastic releases poisonous chemicals when it burns.
senses.example_zh: 释放人质 // 塑料燃烧时释放出有毒物质。

senses.def_en: to free (sb.) from a duty, responsibility, etc.
senses.example: The club is releasing some of its older players.
senses.example_zh: 俱乐部正在解聘一些老队员。

senses.example: He refused to release her arm.
senses.example_zh: 他不肯放开她的手臂。

senses.def_en: to make (sth.) available to the public
senses.example: The names of the delegates were not released to the press.
senses.example_zh: 代表们的名字没有向报界公布。

senses.example: the release of the prisoners // the release of carbon dioxide
senses.example_zh: 释放囚犯 // 二氧化碳的排放

senses.example: Crying gave some emotional release.
senses.example_zh: 哭泣缓解了些痛苦的情绪。

senses.example: October 22nd is the date set for the report's release.
senses.example_zh: 定于10月22日发布该报告。

senses.def_en: to allow sb. to leave a hospital, prison, etc.
senses.example: The judge discharged the prisoner.
senses.example_zh: 法官把囚犯释放了。

senses.def_en: to send out (a liquid, gas or waste material)
senses.example: Some factories in this region still discharge waste water into the river. // The river discharges into the sea.
senses.example_zh: 这一地区有些工厂仍把废水排入河中。// 那条河注入大海。

senses.example: The aircraft discharged its passengers.
senses.example_zh: 飞机卸下乘客。

senses.example: Lightning is caused by clouds discharging electricity.
senses.example_zh: 闪电是由云层放电造成的。

senses.example: Patients' needs after discharge from hospital will be monitored.
senses.example_zh: 出院后病人的需求将得到监控。

senses.example: the discharge of toxic waste into the sea
senses.example_zh: 有毒废物向海里排放

senses.def_en: to decide not to think about or consider (sth. or sb.)
senses.example: A good few people dismiss the idea as sheer nonsense.
senses.example_zh: 不少人认为这种想法是胡说八道。

senses.def_en: to end the employment or service of sb.
senses.example: Bryant claims he was unfairly dismissed from his post.
senses.example_zh: 布赖恩特声称自己被无理免职。

senses.def_en: to send (sb.) away or allow them to leave
senses.example: The chairman dismissed the meeting.
senses.example_zh: 主席宣布散会。

senses.example: The justice dismissed his evidence out of hand.
senses.example_zh: 法官当即驳回了他的证据。

derivatives: dismissal n. 解散;解雇;不予考虑

senses.def_en: to officially force sb. to leave a school or organization
senses.example: The pupil was expelled for stealing.
senses.example_zh: 那学生因偷窃被开除了。

senses.example: Russia lately expelled a US diplomat from its land.
senses.example_zh: 俄罗斯最近驱逐了一名美国外交官。

senses.example: The lungs expel carbon dioxide when we exhale.
senses.example_zh: 当我们呼气时，肺排出了二氧化碳。
